name: Oppland (Norway, 1989–2019)

colors.symbolism: the forests and inland landscape of Oppland
silver (argent) for the trees and fish

description: A green field charged with two silver birch trees in chief and a silver fish in base.
history: Granted to Oppland county on 12 May 1989. The county was abolished on 1 January 2020 when it was merged with Hedmark into the new Innlandet county.

features.symbolism: heraldic banner of the county arms: a green field charged with stylised silver birch trees and a fish, evoking Oppland's forests and lakes
the birch is emblematic of the inland forests of Oppland
the lakes and rivers of Oppland (notably Mjosa, Norway's largest lake)
